short: Shrinking a model by storing its weights in fewer bits (e.g. 16-bit to 4-bit).
definition: Quantization reduces the numerical precision of a model's weights (and sometimes activations) — for example from 16-bit floats to 8-bit or 4-bit integers. This cuts memory use and speeds up inference, usually with only a small accuracy loss. Common schemes include GPTQ, AWQ, FP8, and NF4. It is what lets large models run on a single consumer GPU.
